documents.content: In Year 5 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Processing and analysing data and information), students learn to: Use knowledge of scientific concepts to draw conclusions that are consistent with evidence

metadata.subject: Science
metadata.substrand: Processing and analysing data and information

documents.content: In Year 5 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Evaluating), students learn to: Evaluate conclusions, including identifying sources of uncertainty and possible alternative explanations, and describe specific ways to improve the quality of the data

In Year 5 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Evaluating), students learn to: Critically analyse the validity of information in primary and secondary sources and evaluate the approaches used to solve problems

In Year 5 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Communicating), students learn to: Communicate scientific ideas and information for a particular purpose, including constructing evidence-based arguments and using appropriate scientific language, conventions and representations
In Year 6 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Questioning and predicting), students learn to: Formulate questions or hypotheses that can be investigated scientifically

In Year 6 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Planning and conducting), students learn to: Plan, select and use appropriate investigation types, including field work and laboratory experimentation, to collect reliable data; assess risk and address ethical issues associated with these methods

In Year 6 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Planning and conducting), students learn to: Select and use appropriate equipment, including digital technologies, to collect and record data systematically and accurately

In Year 6 Science, under Science Inquiry Skills (Processing and analysing data and information), students learn to: Analyse patterns and trends in data, including describing relationships between variables and identifying inconsistencies
